title: Chalcas glenieii
taxonomy: Chalcas glenieii is a plant species within the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Chalcas, which is a group of flowering plants. The classification of Chalcas glenieii places it in the order Sapindales, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry order. The class to which Chalcas glenieii belongs is Magnoliopsida, which includes most flowering plants with a single cotyledon. Further classification includes its kingdom, Plantae, indicating its status as a plant species.

intro: Chalcas glenieii, also known as Chalcas, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly found in the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Chalcas glenieii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which have been identified as responsible for its therapeutic properties.
